title: Ensure ‘Log_min_error_statement’ Database Flag for Cloud  SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘Error’ or Stricter

input_row.title: Ensure ‘Log_min_error_statement’ Database Flag for Cloud  SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘Error’ or Stricter
input_row.description: The log_min_error_statement flag defines the minimum message severity level that are considered as an error statement. Messages for error statements are logged with the SQL statement. Valid values include (from lowest to highest severity) DEBUG5, DEBUG4, DEBUG3, DEBUG2, DEBUG1, INFO, NOTICE, WARNING, ERROR, LOG, FATAL, and PANIC. Each severity level includes the subsequent levels mentioned above. Ensure a value of ERROR or stricter is set.
input_row.rationale: Auditing helps in troubleshooting operational problems and also permits forensic analysis. If log_min_error_statement is not set to the correct value, messages may not be classified as error messages appropriately. Considering general log messages as error messages would make is difficult to find actual errors and considering only stricter severity levels as error messages may skip actual errors to log their SQL statements. The log_min_error_statement flag should be set to ERROR or stricter. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview . Go to Configuration card 4. Under Database flags, check the value of log_min_error_statement flag is configured as to ERROR or stricter. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify the value of log_min_error_statement is set to ERROR or stricter. gcloud sql instances describe --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance for which you want to enable the database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag log_min_error_statement from the drop-down menu and set appropriate value. 6. Click Save to save your changes. 7. Confirm your changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the log_min_error_statement database flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch --database-flags log_min_error_statement= Note: This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags you want set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("=").
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.additional_information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note: some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note: Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_min_error_statement_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program will check the configuration of the `log_min_error_statement` database flag for all PostgreSQL instances in Google Cloud SQL.
- **API Calls:** The program will utilize the Google Cloud SQL API or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to make the following calls:
  - `gcloud sql instances describe` to retrieve instance settings, specifically checking the list of database flags.
- **GCP CLI Command:** 
  - The command `gcloud sql instances describe --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement")|.value'` will be executed to extract the value of the `log_min_error_statement` flag for each instance.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** The program will target all Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instances in the project.
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:** The specific configuration property being validated is the `log_min_error_statement` flag.
- **Pass Criteria:** The value of `log_min_error_statement` must be set to either `ERROR` or a stricter level (e.g., `FATAL`, `PANIC`).
- **Fail Criteria:** If the value of `log_min_error_statement` is not present, set to `LOG`, `WARNING`, `NOTICE`, `INFO`, or any level lower than `ERROR`, then it is considered a compliance failure.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The task of checking the `log_min_error_statement` flag can be effectively automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud).
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Feasibility:** The use of the `gcloud sql instances describe` command is appropriate for retrieving the configuration of Cloud SQL instances, specifically the database flags. This aligns with GCP SDK capabilities and is a valid approach for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are mostly correct. The use of `gcloud sql instances describe` is appropriate to fetch the instance settings. However, the command provided in the assessment lacks a specific instance name or project specification, which might be necessary for accurate execution.
- **GCP CLI Command Validity:** The command is valid but needs refinement. It should include a specific instance name and possibly a project ID to ensure it targets the correct resources.
- **Program Name Convention:** The program name `gcp_sql_instance_log_min_error_statement_configured` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity. It does not explicitly indicate the compliance check aspect or the severity level being verified.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Command Refinement:** The `gcloud sql instances describe` command should specify an instance name and project ID for clarity and accuracy. Example: `gcloud sql instances describe INSTANCE_NAME --project=PROJECT_ID --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement") | .value'`.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_error_statement_compliance_check` for better clarity and adherence to naming conventions.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using the Google Cloud SQL Admin API directly for more granular control and possibly integrating with a monitoring or alerting system for continuous compliance checks.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured, and the technical approach is largely accurate. The necessary tools and commands are correctly identified, and the compliance criteria are clear.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modify the Command:** Include specific instance and project identifiers in the `gcloud` command to ensure it targets the correct resources.
- **Rename the Program:** Update the program name to `gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_error_statement_compliance_check` for improved clarity and adherence to naming conventions.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the task of checking the `log_min_error_statement` flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances can be automated using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The `gcloud sql instances describe` command is capable of retrieving the necessary configuration details, specifically the database flags, which makes automation feasible. The task involves checking a configuration setting that is exposed through GCP APIs, allowing for an efficient and reliable automated solution.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_sql_postgresql_log_min_error_statement_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instances
- **API/CLI Method:** Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command: `gcloud sql instances describe <instance-name> --project=<project-id> --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement")|.value'`
- **Properties to Check:** The `log_min_error_statement` flag value for each PostgreSQL instance.
- **Pass Criteria:** The value of `log_min_error_statement` must be set to `ERROR` or a stricter level (e.g., `FATAL`, `PANIC`).
- **Fail Criteria:** If the value of `log_min_error_statement` is set to `LOG`, `WARNING`, `NOTICE`, `INFO`, or any level lower than `ERROR`, it indicates non-compliance.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested refining the command to include specific instance names and project IDs, which has been incorporated into the final automation details.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
